evidence_quotes.quote: privacy due to the required ID's.
websites that were previously accessible by children, such as Pornhub, are not anymore

paragraphs.text: With the advent of UK's and some US states age restrictions on adult content, there is a concern of privacy due to the required ID's. But more importantly these changes mean that websites that were previously accessible by children, such as Pornhub, are not anymore, this is a short sighted attempt at "protecting the children" that doesn't even conceive about the fact that children will try to find alternatives, furthermore private chats. While children shouldn't have access to adult social media, said access can be limited to just making sure they cannot post any content of their own, such as comments or private messages. Websites now accessible by children such as pornhub.com or e621.net should not be limited in access but adult social media like X (formerly twitter) should due to improper moderation of it's users. There are websites much worse than X such as the ones that get spun up with illegal content on a regular basis (the ones i'd consider to be a whackamole game as they have constantly changing domains and tend to be hosted by a single person). I don't really have closing thoughts so I'll end it here.
